description:
## Goal

Three long-lived periodic fibers in `src/services/app_bootstrap.cr`
use plain `sleep` instead of the shared `InterruptibleSleep`
helper that was extracted in `quickhea-aiu`:

- `start_watchdog` (line 93): `sleep(QuickHeadlines::Constants::WATCHDOG_INTERVAL_SECONDS)`
- `start_cleanup_scheduler` (line 245): `sleep(@cleanup_interval)`
- `start_ws_janitor` (line 292): `sleep(@ws_janitor_interval)`

The shutdown sequence in `src/quickheadlines.cr` (lines 58, 79, 83)
also uses plain `sleep(5.seconds)`, `sleep(10.seconds)`,
`sleep(30.seconds)`.

## Why

Plain `sleep` blocks for the full duration regardless of
`QuickHeadlines.shutting_down?`. The `InterruptibleSleep.sleep`
helper chunks the wait into smaller intervals and checks the
shutdown flag between each. The watchdog, cleanup, and janitor
fibers can each delay shutdown by their full sleep interval
(typically 30s-1h) even when a shutdown signal is pending.

## Why this is part of the memory review

The shutdown path is not the leak itself, but it is the
"could be a leak" path: if a long-lived fiber captures a
large object in its closure, the plain `sleep` keeps the
fiber alive, the fiber keeps the closure alive, and the closure
keeps the object alive. Switching to `InterruptibleSleep.sleep`
gives the GC a chance to reclaim those objects when the
fiber exits at shutdown.

## Acceptance

- All four `sleep` sites above use `InterruptibleSleep.sleep`
- Build + spec + ameba clean
- Boot smoke test: send SIGINT, server shuts down within 30s
  (currently can take up to 30+1h)
title: Replace plain sleep with InterruptibleSleep in long-lived periodic fibers
